notes: Official Claude Code docs are the source of truth.
The two reading.sh articles are secondary guidance for real-world patterns and async trade-offs.
Async is documented in the official hooks reference as of 2026-04-10.
Only command hooks support async execution.

events.name: SessionStart
events.description: Runs when a session begins or resumes.
events.async_guidance: Use sync when you need context, environment, or bootstrap work before Claude continues.

events.name: InstructionsLoaded
events.description: Runs when a CLAUDE.md file or .claude/rules markdown file is loaded into context.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync when you need to enforce policy or inject context. Use async only for passive audit work.

events.name: UserPromptSubmit
events.description: Runs when the user submits a prompt, before Claude processes it.
events.async_guidance: Usually keep sync because this event is most valuable as an input gate.

events.name: PreToolUse
events.description: Runs before a tool call executes and can block it.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync for safety or policy gates. Async is not useful when you need to block.

events.name: PermissionRequest
events.description: Runs when a permission dialog appears.
events.async_guidance: Usually keep sync because the value of this event is the permission decision.

events.name: PermissionDenied
events.description: Runs when a tool call is denied by the auto mode classifier.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync when you want to suggest a retry or a deterministic recovery path.

events.name: PostToolUse
events.description: Runs after a tool call succeeds.
events.async_guidance: This is the strongest async candidate for formatters, targeted tests, notifications, and metrics.

events.name: PostToolUseFailure
events.description: Runs after a tool call fails.
events.async_guidance: Use async for alerts or logging. Keep sync when the failure handler must shape Claude's next move.

events.name: Notification
events.description: Runs when Claude Code sends a notification.
events.async_guidance: Usually async because this event is mostly about side effects.

events.name: SubagentStart
events.description: Runs when a subagent is spawned.
events.async_guidance: Often async for logging or external coordination. Keep sync only when startup must finish first.

events.name: SubagentStop
events.description: Runs when a subagent finishes.
events.async_guidance: Use sync for completion checks. Use async for logging or notifications.

events.name: TaskCreated
events.description: Runs when a task is being created via TaskCreate.
events.async_guidance: Depends on whether the task creation needs a gate or only an audit trail.

events.name: TaskCompleted
events.description: Runs when a task is being marked as completed.
events.async_guidance: Depends on whether the completion check must gate or only report.

events.name: Stop
events.description: Runs when Claude finishes responding.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync for end-of-turn quality gates. Use async only for passive side effects like notifications.

events.name: StopFailure
events.description: Runs when a turn ends because of an API error.
events.async_guidance: Often async because the main value is audit or alerting, and output is ignored.

events.name: TeammateIdle
events.description: Runs when an agent-team teammate is about to go idle.
events.async_guidance: Depends on whether you need a blocking intervention or only a notification.

events.name: ConfigChange
events.description: Runs when a configuration file changes during a session.
events.async_guidance: Use async for audit logs. Keep sync when a policy decision must block the change.

events.name: CwdChanged
events.description: Runs when the working directory changes.
events.async_guidance: Usually keep sync when the hook updates the environment before the next Bash command.

events.name: FileChanged
events.description: Runs when a watched file changes on disk.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync when the file change must immediately refresh environment state.

events.name: WorktreeCreate
events.description: Runs when a worktree is being created.
events.async_guidance: Usually sync because this event can replace the default worktree behavior.

events.name: WorktreeRemove
events.description: Runs when a worktree is being removed.
events.async_guidance: Often async for cleanup, unless the cleanup must complete before the worktree is considered gone.

events.name: PreCompact
events.description: Runs before context compaction.
events.async_guidance: Usually sync because pre-compaction work often needs to finish before compaction proceeds.

events.name: PostCompact
events.description: Runs after context compaction completes.
events.async_guidance: Use async for passive logging. Keep sync only when the follow-up state must be ready immediately.

events.name: SessionEnd
events.description: Runs when a session terminates.
events.async_guidance: Often async for cleanup or audit. Keep sync when shutdown work must finish first.

events.name: Elicitation
events.description: Runs when an MCP server requests user input during a tool call.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync because the hook can accept, decline, or cancel the elicitation.

events.name: ElicitationResult
events.description: Runs after a user responds to an MCP elicitation, before the response is sent back.
events.async_guidance: Keep sync because the hook can rewrite or block the elicitation response.
